summary:
The stability of dicalcium phosphate monohydrate results in an approximate 37% retention of un-rinsed phosphoric acid due to its insoluble crystal structure. This value corresponds directly to known historical findings regarding washout efficiency for such calcium-phosphate compounds used in pharmaceuticals.  

(Note: Only include content strictly related to the core clinical or scientific rationale behind the answer.)
